title: Unable to assign multiple network card
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

I am trying to assign multiple networks to a container but it takes only one network into consideration.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Setup 1st newtork conf file in /etc/cni/net.d
```
cat /etc/cni/net.d/88-nw1-bridge.conflist 
{
    "cniVersion": "0.3.0",
    "name": "nw1",
    "plugins": [
      {
        "type": "bridge",
        "bridge": "cni0",
        "isGateway": true,
        "ipMasq": true,
        "ipam": {
            "type": "host-local",
            "subnet": "172.16.1.0/24",
            "routes": [
                { "dst": "0.0.0.0/0" }
            ]
        }
      },
      {
        "type": "portmap",
        "capabilities": {
          "portMappings": true
        }
      }
    ]
}

```

2. Setup 2nd network conf file in /etc/cni/net.d
```
cat /etc/cni/net.d/89-nw2-bridge.conflist 
{
    "cniVersion": "0.3.0",
    "name": "nw2",
    "plugins": [
      {
        "type": "bridge",
        "bridge": "cni1",
        "isGateway": true,
        "ipMasq": true,
        "ipam": {
            "type": "host-local",
            "subnet": "192.168.17.0/24",
            "routes": [
                { "dst": "0.0.0.0/0" }
            ]
        }
      },
      {
        "type": "portmap",
        "capabilities": {
          "portMappings": true
        }
      }
    ]
}

```

3. Create the container
```
podman create -t -i      --hostname test2 --systemd=true  --net nw1   --net  nw2     --name test2    oraclelinux:7
```
4. Login to the container and check the network
```
[root@test2 /]# ifconfig
	bash: ifconfig: command not found
	[root@test2 /]# ip a
	1: lo: <LOOPBACK,UP,LOWER_UP> mtu 65536 qdisc noqueue state UNKNOWN group default qlen 1000
	    link/loopback 00:00:00:00:00:00 brd 00:00:00:00:00:00
	    inet 127.0.0.1/8 scope host lo
	       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
	    inet6 ::1/128 scope host 
	       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
	3: eth0@if80: <BROADCAST,MULTICAST,UP,LOWER_UP> mtu 1500 qdisc noqueue state UP group default 
	    link/ether 6e:37:ed:86:db:51 brd ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff link-netnsid 0
	    inet 192.168.17.5/24 brd 192.168.17.255 scope global eth0
	       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
	    inet6 fe80::6c37:edff:fe86:db51/64 scope link 
	       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
```
**Describe the results you received:**
PODMAN container got only one network.

**Describe the results you expected:**
PODMAN container must have 2 network cards based 


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.4.4
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.12.9
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
debug:
  compiler: gc
  git commit: ""
  go version: go1.12.9
  podman version: 1.4.4
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.9.0
  Conmon:
    package: podman-1.4.4-4.0.1.el7.x86_64
    path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 0.3.0, commit: unknown'
  Distribution:
    distribution: '"ol"'
    version: "7.8"
  MemFree: 872071168
  MemTotal: 16535801856
  OCIRuntime:
    package: runc-1.0.0-65.rc8.el7.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.1-dev'
  SwapFree: 8579694592
  SwapTotal: 8589930496
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 2
  hostname: raconpodman
  kernel: 4.14.35-1902.305.4.el7uek.x86_64
  os: linux
  rootless: false
  uptime: 189h 20m 34.05s (Approximately 7.88 days)
registries:
  blocked: null
  insecure: null
  search:
  - container-registry.oracle.com
  - quay.io
  - docker.io
store:
  ConfigFile: /etc/containers/storage.conf
  ContainerStore:
    number: 4
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions: null
  GraphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: xfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "false"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  ImageStore:
    number: 11
  RunRoot: /var/run/containers/storage
  VolumePath: /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-1.4.4-4.0.1.el7.x86_64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

NO

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):** 
It is a virtual machine.

comments.body: Please try a more recent Podman. I'm 99% sure this issue is fixed on 2.0 and higher.

It's an issue as reported in v2.2.1.

workaround is to use: `--network=nw1,nw2`
